boundary_status_reviews.reasoning: C3S Tier 1 data confirms three-year average 2023-2025 exceeded 1.5°C for first time and March 2026 at 1.48°C, with projection of sustained breach by February 2034 — a nine-year acceleration from 2015 baseline. This reinforces structural acceleration within already beyond-boundary status. No evidence for status change.

Ship sulfur regulation reduces aerosols, increasing GBR thermal stress by 5-10% via novel pathway. Reinforces continuing trajectory; no status change.

No new ozone data retrieved. Stable trajectory holds.
El Niño SST anomalies and AMOC carbon feedback (+47-83 ppm CO₂) imply continued pressure on aragonite states. No new data; continuing trajectory.

analyst_briefing: Climate change boundary shows structural acceleration with C3S-confirmed 2023-2025 1.5°C exceedance and 2034 breach projection, nine years ahead of 2015 baseline — no status change from beyond_boundary but flags NDC failure. Tipping assessments: AMOC and coral show trajectory_acceleration (multi-mooring decline, El Niño bleaching window + aerosol pathway); Amazon continuation under drought precursor; ice_sheets/permafrost no_signal. Cascade patterns escalate in Central America Dry Corridor (food insecurity → displacement, scem signal) with medium confidence; SE Asia/northern S America continue at medium/low. Escalations: gmm for AMOC carbon tail risk (HIGH) and El Niño commodities (MEDIUM); scem for displacement-conflict nexus (MEDIUM); wdm/fcw for governance/disinfo risks (MEDIUM). No contested findings; all sources Tier 1/2 verified. Priority watch: El Niño intensity, Amazon fires, AMOC moorings, coral alerts next 30-90 days.
